hook.text: Two factories claim equal consistency. How do you test if one is actually more variable?

buildup.text: The F-distribution compares variances. Is one group more spread out than the other?

discovery.text: Divide one variance by the other. A large F-ratio means the groups have genuinely different spreads.

twist.text: It powers ANOVA, which compares means across three or more groups at once — a statistical workhorse.

climax.text: Drug trials, crop experiments, and quality control all depend on the F-distribution daily.

punchline.text: Comparing variability? The F-ratio is your tool.

quiz.question: What does the F-distribution compare?
options: The variances (spread) of two or more groups
